description: V&A golden search cases (12): the per-museum accuracy north-star for the Victoria and Albert Museum rows in the merged artifact. Authored 2026-07-05 against the on_display_at=southken on-view set (~58k records at measurement time), all verified live via the V&A Collections API v2 search endpoint before being written down (systemNumber, title, artist, gallery all confirmed on-display). Tiers mirror the Met set: autocomplete = local FTS top-8; full = all-results; llm = relaxed-FTS stand-in for the interpret tier.

cases.note: V&A icon; O9138, British Galleries Room 57
largest historic carpet on public display; Room 42, The Jameel Gallery
Cast Courts plaster cast; apostrophe-free query must match (normalizeQuery strips punctuation)
Dale Chihuly glass sculpture at the Cromwell Road entrance
Dante Gabriel Rossetti design; artist token disambiguates the common noun
the Raphael Cartoons, Room 48a; artist + classification tokens land on different columns of the same row
two on-view works share this title (Nollekens sculptor, Room 22; Jüchtzer modeller, Room 1) — either satisfies the case
Silver, Room 65. NOT '... cup silver': V&A search-tier rows carry no medium text, so a 'silver' token has no searchable source and zeroes the strict-AND tier

cases.query: an ornate four poster bed too big for its own bedroom
cases.note: descriptive phrasing for the icon; 'bed' is the surviving anchor token after stopword relaxation

cases.query: an ironwork choir screen door at the entrance to a chapel
cases.note: medium + classification phrasing ('Choir screen' is objectType, not in the title itself)

cases.query: a giant ancient roman stone column with a carved spiral relief
cases.note: descriptive phrasing for the Cast Courts cast; 'column' is the anchor token
